---
title: Penguin report
subtitle: "`r paste(params$location, params$year)`"
params:
  location: "Biscoe"       
  year: 2008 
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    embed-resources: true
    template-partials: 
      - _extensions/banner-with-img/title-block.html
execute:
  echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
author: "Scottish Trauma Audit Group"
css: _extensions/phs-html-quarto/phs_style.css
title-block-banner: "#3F3685"
title-image: _extensions/phs-html-quarto/phs-logo.png
---

## Example report

Reporting on all penguins on `r params$location` island in `r params$year`.

### Overview
```{r}
library(bslib)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggiraph)
library(DT)

dat <- penguins |>
  filter(year == params$year,
         island == params$location) |>
  select(species, sex, bill_len:body_mass)

dat |>
  datatable(extensions = c('Select', 'SearchPanes'),
              selection = 'none',
              rownames = FALSE,
              options = list(dom = 'Prtip',
                             searchPanes = list(
                               controls = FALSE,
                               viewCount = FALSE
                             ),
                             columnDefs = list(
                               list(searchPanes = list(show = FALSE),
                                    targets = 2:5)
                             ),
                             pageLength = 5)
            )
```

### Flipper length by body mass

```{r include=FALSE}
my_plot <- function(sx){
  filter(dat, sex == sx) |>
    ggplot(aes(body_mass, flipper_len, colour=species,
               data_id = species)) +
    geom_point_interactive()
}

set_girafe_defaults(
  opts_hover = opts_hover(css = "opacity:0.8"),
  opts_hover_inv = opts_hover_inv(css = "opacity:0.4"))

# due to some odd behaviour in Quarto, girafe needs to be called (silently) before it works in a tabset 
girafe(my_plot("male"))
```


```{r}
#| panel: tabset
#| output: asis
#| keep-md: true

plots <- list(
  "Male" = my_plot("male"),
  "Female" = my_plot("female"))

purrr::iwalk(plots, ~ {
  cat('## ', .y, '\n\n')
  
  print(htmltools::div(girafe(.x)))
  
  cat('\n\n')
  
})
```
